tool_args_summary: {"statement": "If the rocba host was compromised or used for unauthorized data access, the Sigma ingest detections will identify high-severity activity such as suspicious logons, data staging, or exfiltration tooling to anchor the investigation.", "specialist": "LOG", "evidence_expected": ["High-severity Sigma detection records related to logon/access, suspicious execution, cloud transfer, or data staging"]}
{"limit": 20}

{"text": "Sigma detection samples show failed Security logon attempts for ADMINISTRATOR from 136.144.176.69 and for administrator from 213.202.233.90, both with event_id=4625 and LogonType=3.", "cited_spans": [{"record_id": 2514145, "span_text": "SIGMA DETECTION level=medium rule=Failed Logon Attempt tags=credential_access,t1110 event_id=4625 channel=Security TargetUserName=ADMINISTRATOR SubjectUserName=- IpAddress=136.144.176.69 WorkstationName=- LogonType=3"}, {"record_id": 2514142, "span_text": "SIGMA DETECTION level=medium rule=Failed Logon Attempt tags=credential_access,t1110 event_id=4625 channel=Security TargetUserName=administrator SubjectUserName=- IpAddress=213.202.233.90 WorkstationName=Remmina LogonType=3"}]}

{"confidence": "HIGH", "justification": "The cited Sigma records explicitly identify Security event_id=4625 failed logon detections tagged credential_access,t1110 against administrator variants, including source IP addresses and LogonType=3. The detections are samples from a very large set of medium detections, so they establish hostile authentication pressure but not successful access on their own.", "observation_id": "O-001", "text": "The host experienced credential-guessing or password-spray style failed network logon attempts against administrator-named accounts from external IP addresses; this anchors Q4 as attempted network logon access but does not by itself prove compromise.", "what_would_change_this_confidence": "Confidence would decrease if the underlying Security records contradicted the Sigma extraction, or increase in scope if raw successful logons from the same IPs or immediately following these failures were found."}
{"hypothesis_id": "H-001", "evidence_audit_ids": ["O-001", "I-001"]}

{"statement": "If the failed network logon activity led to actual access, Security or Terminal Services logs will show successful logons to a local account on the rocba host during the same November 2020 period.", "specialist": "LOG", "evidence_expected": ["Security event_id=4624 records with LogonType 10 or 3", "Terminal Services session connection records naming a user or source address"]}

{"query": "event_id=4624 AND LogonType=3", "source_tool": "evtx:Security", "limit": 20}
{"query": "RemoteDesktop OR TerminalServices OR Session", "source_tool": null, "limit": 20}
{"query": "4624 AND LogonType AND 10", "source_tool": "evtx:Security", "limit": 50}
{"query": "4624 AND LogonType AND 3", "source_tool": "evtx:Security", "limit": 50}
{"query": "4624 AND LogonType AND 10", "source_tool": null, "limit": 50}
{"query": "4624 AND LogonType AND 3", "source_tool": null, "limit": 50}
{"query": "fred.rocba@outlook.com AND LogonType AND 10", "source_tool": "evtx:Security", "limit": 20}

{"hypothesis_id": "H-002", "evidence_audit_ids": ["O-002", "I-002"]}

{"section": "findings", "initial_hypothesis": "H-001: If the rocba host was compromised or used for unauthorized data access, Sigma ingest detections would identify high-severity activity such as suspicious logons, data staging, or exfiltration tooling to anchor the investigation.", "text": "Key Questions addressed: Q1 accounts accessed: srl-h, fredr, and fred.rocba@outlook.com are supported by revised observation O-006. Q2 data taken/targeted: SRL project folders, staging-like folders, and SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst are supported by O-003. Q3 transfer destination: G:\\My Drive and OneDrive path references and cloud-client executions are supported by revised observation O-007, but completed upload/sync is not proven. Q4 access method: failed credential attacks, NTLM network logons, and RDP interactive logon evidence are supported by O-001 and O-006. Q5 timing: the November 2020 incident sequence is supported by O-005, with record timestamps in the case index linking logon, data-path access, cloud-client execution,

history.statement: If the rocba host was compromised or used for unauthorized data access, the Sigma ingest detections will identify high-severity activity such as suspicious logons, data staging, or exfiltration tooling to anchor the investigation.
evidence_expected: High-severity Sigma detection records related to logon/access, suspicious execution, cloud transfer, or data staging

history.statement: If the failed network logon activity led to actual access, Security or Terminal Services logs will show successful logons to a local account on the rocba host during the same November 2020 period.
evidence_expected: Security event_id=4624 records with LogonType 10 or 3
Terminal Services session connection records naming a user or source address

history.statement: If data was staged or accessed for removal, the user's Recent LNK or JumpList records will show SRL project files, exports, or collected folders opened around the successful November 2020 logons.
evidence_expected: Recent LNK records for SRL project files or exports
JumpList records showing access to sensitive project folders or staged collections
MFT/USN records for archive or export files

history.statement: If the accessed SRL project and email data was transferred off-host, transfer evidence will point to cloud-sync locations such as Google Drive/My Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, or external-volume paths in LNK/SRUM/prefetch records around 2020-11-14.
evidence_expected: LNK or JumpList targets under G:\My Drive or OneDrive/Dropbox paths for the accessed data
Prefetch execution for GoogleDriveFS/OneDrive/Dropbox near the activity
SRUM network usage records for cloud-sync applications

history.statement: If the incident timeline can be reconstructed, the case index will show a sequence linking successful November 2020 logons, SRL data access, cloud-sync client execution, and cleanup-related execution.
evidence_expected: LNK records for SRL data access around November 2020
